How I use it.</p><div><hr></div><h2 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Claude Chat</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§claude-chat" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/claude-chat" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h2><p>This is where everyone starts.</p><p><span>1. Go to claude.ai</span><br><span>2. Open the app</span><br><span>3. Type…</span></p><p>But Chat has 12 built-in tools. Most people use 3.</p><p>Here is the full list:</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Models + Extended Thinking</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§models-extended-thinking" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/models-extended-thinking" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p><strong>Opus 4.6</strong><span> is the most capable model. 1M token context window. Use it for strategy, legal analysis, financial modelling, and anything with multiple variables.</span></p><p><strong>Sonnet 4.6</strong><span> handles 90% of your daily work. Faster. Cheaper. I use this for everything unless I have a reason not to.</span></p><p><strong>Haiku 4.5</strong><span> is the lightweight option. Fast and cheap. Good for quick answers and bulk tasks where depth does not matter.</span></p><p>My rule: start with Sonnet. Switch to Opus when the task demands it. Most people leave Opus on permanently and burn through their usage limit by Wednesday.</p><p>Below the model selector you will see Extended Thinking. Toggle it on. Claude reasons through your problem step by step before responding. Shows its working. Breaks complex questions into parts.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" The difference is like asking someone to answer immediately versus giving them 5 minutes to think. Turn on Extended Thinking and Web Search at the same time. Two clicks that change everything.</p><p>How to access: model dropdown at the top of any conversation.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Memory</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§memory" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/memory" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Claude remembers you across conversations. Free for all users since March 2026.</p><p>Two settings to enable in Settings → Capabilities:</p><p><strong>Search and reference chats </strong><span>→ Claude searches your past conversations for relevant context.</span></p><p><strong>Generate memory from chat history </strong><span>→ Claude remembers details about you across all chats and Projects.</span></p><p>Turn both on. This is what makes Claude feel like it knows you.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" class="caption-QiPycG">You have spent months teaching ChatGPT who you are. That context feels impossible to leave behind.</p></div><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-gap-16 pc-paddingTop-0 pc-paddingBottom-0 pc-alignItems-center pc-reset"><a href="https://charliehills.substack.com/p/claude-code-cowork-the-complete-guide" class="pencraft pc-reset align-center-y7ZD4w line-height-20-t4M0El font-text-qe4AeH size-13-hZTUKr weight-medium-fw81nC reset-IxiVJZ"><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-gap-8 pc-alignItems-center pc-reset link-HREYZo"><span class="pencraft pc-reset color-accent-BVX_7M line-height-20-t4M0El font-text-qe4AeH size-14-MLPa7j weight-semibold-uqA4FV reset-IxiVJZ">Read full story</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-arrow-right"><path d="M5 12h14"></path><path d="m12 5 7 7-7 7"></path></svg></div></a></div></div></div><p><strong>Clean up your memory regularly.</strong></p><p>Claude sometimes misinterprets things and adds random details about you. Old projects you are no longer working on, incorrect assumptions about your role, outdated preferences. Go into your memory, read through it, and remove anything that is wrong or stale.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" Claude saves it to memory, as long as you have the settings enabled.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" Review it every few weeks. Remove what is outdated. Add what is missing. The cleaner your memory, the less time you spend correcting Claude in every conversation.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Web Search</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§web-search" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/web-search" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Claude searches the web mid-conversation and pulls live data into its response.</p><p>Two ways to trigger it:</p><p><span>→ Toggle it on in Settings (stays on for every chat)</span><br><span>→ Or type “search the web for...” in your prompt (one-off)</span></p><p>I keep mine toggled off by default to save tokens. When I need it, I type “search the web” directly in my message. That’s faster than enabling it, and I only burn the tokens when the task needs live data.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" Claude pulls live data AND reasons through it step by step. Two toggles that change the quality of every response.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Deep Research</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§deep-research" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/deep-research" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>This is different from Web Search. Deep Research runs a full multi-source investigation. Claude searches dozens of sources, cross-references them, and returns a structured report.</p><p>How to use:</p><p><span>→ Click the Research button next to the message bar</span><br><span>→ Enter your topic</span><br><span>→ Wait for the full report (takes a few minutes)</span></p><p>I sometimes use it before every newsletter. I give it a topic, it comes back with data points, competitor examples, and source links.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" strategies LinkedIn creators with 100K+ followers used in Q1 2026, with specific examples and engagement data” → something I can publish from.</p><p>Tip: Deep Research burns through tokens fast. Use it for the research phase, then switch to a normal chat to write from the findings. Do not research and write in the same thread.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>File Upload</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§file-upload" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/file-upload" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Drag PDFs, images, spreadsheets, or documents directly into the chat. Claude reads the full content.</p><p>How to use:</p><p><span>→ Click the paperclip icon</span><br><span>→ Or drag files directly into the chat window</span><br><span>→ Works with PDFs, CSVs, images, Word docs, and more</span></p><p>Upload 2 or 3 documents at once and ask Claude to compare them. “Here are three competitor landing pages. You iterate inside the chat.</p><p>Tip: say “make this an Artifact” if Claude gives you code in the chat instead of building it. Sometimes it defaults to showing code. Telling it to make an Artifact forces it to render the live, interactive version.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Interactive Charts</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§interactive-charts" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/interactive-charts" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Added March 12. renders it inline in the conversation</span><br><span>→ You interact with it directly in the chat</span></p><p>This is different from Analysis Tool. Analysis Tool runs code on your data. Interactive charts are visual outputs Claude builds from any information, not files only. Ask Claude to “create an org chart for my team” or “visualise this process as a flowchart” and it renders it live.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" list.</p><p>Two ways to trigger it:</p><p><span>→ Claude decides to use it automatically</span><br><span>→ Or type “AskUserQuestion:” in your prompt to force it</span></p><p><span>“Which format do you want?” → clickable buttons.</span><br><span>“Rank these in order of priority” → drag and drop.</span><br><span>“Which of these apply?” → multi-select checkboxes.</span></p><p>Tip: if Claude asks you a question in plain text and you would prefer clickable options, say “give me options to choose from.” Claude switches to the interactive format. Works the other way too. If you want to type a freeform answer instead of picking from options, ignore the buttons and type your response.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Projects</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§projects" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/projects" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Save files and instructions once. Every new chat inside a Project starts with full context. Claude reads everything before you type a word.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" x2="10" y1="21" y2="14"></line></svg></button></div></div></div></a></figure></div><p>How to set up:</p><p><span>→ Click “Create Project” in the left sidebar</span><br><span>→ Upload your key files and write custom instructions</span><br><span>→ Start new chats inside the Project</span></p><p>I run 12 active Projects. One per client, one for this newsletter, one for LinkedIn content. Each has a custom instruction, 3 to 5 reference files, and zero filler.</p><p>Most people do everything in one Project. I do the opposite.</p><p>I take a piece of content and move it through different Projects depending on what I need. A newsletter becomes a LinkedIn post, which becomes an infographic, which becomes an email subject line.</p><p>Each Project has its own custom instructions, tone rules, and reference files. The output is tailored every time without me re-explaining anything.</p><p>I switch between Projects throughout the day depending on the task.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a Projects cache your files once. Upload once. Reference forever.</p><p><em>Paid subscribers: scroll to the bottom of this newsletter for my full newsletter Project instructions. Copy them directly into your own setup.</em></p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Skills</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§skills" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/skills" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Projects are great when you need reference material and baked-in context. up:</p><p><span>→ Go to Settings and enable Code Execution</span><br><span>→ Browse the pre-built Skills library</span><br><span>→ Install a Skill or create your own with a SKILL.md file</span></p><p>I have Skills for newsletter writing, carousel creation, and client reporting.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" Skills. You do not need to type out a SKILL.md file yourself.</p><p>Here is how it works in practice:</p><p><span>→ Explain the problem you are trying to solve</span><br><span>→ Provide reference material of what you want</span><br><span>→ Claude creates the Skill for you</span></p><p>Then you test it and refine it over time through natural language in chat. “Make the tone more casual.” “Add a step where you check for spelling.” “Always include a CTA at the end.” Claude updates the Skill based on your feedback.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" I showed Claude a few examples of the style I wanted, described the dimensions and brand colours, and he wrote the full Skill. I have refined it a few times since, through conversation, and now it produces consistent results every time I run it.</p><p><strong>Advanced: chaining multiple Skills inside a Project</strong></p><p>You can set up a Project that calls multiple Skills as part of one process.</p><p><span>→ Create separate Skills for each stage (researcher, writer, reviewer)</span><br><span>→ Upload the empty SKILL.md files into your Project</span><br><span>→ Tell Claude in the Project instructions which Skill to call at each step</span></p><p>One prompt kicks off the entire chain. Claude researches, writes, and reviews, all following different Skills in sequence.</p><p><span>I do not personally follow this approach yet, but my co-founder at </span><a href="http://www.linked-agency.com">Linked Agency </a><span>recently started using it for client work and it has been a standout for outsourcing repetitive deliverables where the process stays the same but the content changes each week.</span></p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Connectors (MCP)</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§connectors-mcp" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/connectors-mcp" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Connect Slack, Google Drive, Notion, Gmail, and 50+ tools. Claude searches them mid-chat.</p><p>How to connect:</p><p><span>→ Click the connectors icon in any chat</span><br><span>→ Browse and authorise the tools you use</span><br><span>→ Claude pulls context without you uploading anything</span></p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" Last week Claude found a client brief from November I had forgotten existed. Pulled it from Drive in 3 seconds.</p><p>Tip: Connectors do not sync live. Claude searches on demand. Think of it as search, not a dashboard. Also, every connected tool adds to your token usage per message. Only connect the tools you use regularly.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Claude in Chrome</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§claude-in-chrome" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/claude-in-chrome" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>A browsing agent inside your Chrome browser. You walk through a task once and Claude remembers the steps.</p><p>How it works:</p><p><span>→ Click Record in the Chrome extension</span><br><span>→ Do the task manually while Claude watches</span><br><span>→ Claude captures every click, every navigation, every action</span><br><span>→ When you are done, it creates a reusable shortcut</span></p><div Weekly browser reports, daily form checks, regular data pulls from dashboards that have no API.</p><p>Tip: Start by recording a simple task you do every week in the browser. Something repetitive that takes 5 minutes. Once you see Claude replay it without you touching anything, you will find more things to record.</p><div><hr></div><h2 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Claude Cowork</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§claude-cowork" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/claude-cowork" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h2><p>Cowork is where Claude moves from answering questions to doing work on your machine. It reads your files, creates real documents, and delivers finished outputs into your folders.</p><p>If Chat is the brain, Cowork is the hands.</p><p>You need the Claude Desktop app. Mac and Windows. Pro and Max plans.</p><p><span>→ Open Claude Desktop.</span><br><span>→ Click the Cowork tab.</span></p><p>That is your starting point.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a Word docs, spreadsheets, PDFs, presentations. Real files, not copies inside a chat window.</p><p>How to start:</p><p><span>→ Open Claude Desktop</span><br><span>→ Click the Cowork tab</span><br><span>→ Point it at a folder and give it a task</span></p><p><span>“Read everything in this folder and create a summary report.”</span><br><span>“Take these 5 client briefs and create a comparison table.”</span><br><span>“Rename and organise these 200 files by date and type.”</span></p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" If Claude is running a workflow and you want to add something or redirect it, jump in and it folds your instruction into the current run. No need to start again.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" across multiple sources at once</span><br><span>→ Anything sequential that takes too long</span></p><p>Tip: be specific about the output you want upfront. There is less back and forth when sub-agents are running in parallel, so the clarity needs to be in the original prompt.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post">Plugins<div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§plugins" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/plugins" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Skills are individual instruction files. Plugins bundle multiple Skills, connectors, slash commands, and sub-agents into one installable package for an entire role.</p><p>A sales Plugin connects to your CRM, adds prospecting workflows, and gives you /research and /outreach commands. A legal Plugin includes your NDA playbook, document management connector, and /triage-nda.</p><p>Cowork ships with 11 pre-built Plugins covering sales, finance, legal, marketing, HR, engineering, and more.</p><p>Four ways to add them:</p><p><strong>Browse plugins</strong><span> → Pre-built library, one-click install.</span><br><strong>Add marketplace</strong><span> → Your organisation’s private plugins (Team/Enterprise).</span><br><strong>Upload plugin</strong><span> → Drop in one a colleague built.</span><br><strong>Create with Claude</strong><span> → Describe what you want, Claude builds it.</span></p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" Once it works, add a second.</p><p>Build the habit of delegating one recurring task before trying to automate everything at once.</p><p>Where it gets interesting is when you bring Connectors into it.</p><p>Connect your Slack, Google Drive, or Notion, and your Scheduled Tasks stop being static prompts. They become live workflows that pull real data from your tools on a schedule, without you touching anything.</p><p>Here is one of mine.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a Facebook, and YouTube. It collects the 5 most recent posts from each account I track, including captions and engagement metrics, and then compiles everything into a daily content briefing. I open my laptop and it is already done.</p><p>One caveat: your computer needs to be awake for Scheduled Tasks to run. Set them for a time when your laptop is already open, your morning routine, a lunch break, the end of your working day.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post">Dispatch<div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§dispatch" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/dispatch" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Assign Claude a task from your phone. It runs on your desktop while you are away and messages you when the work is done. One continuous conversation across your phone and your computer.</p><p>How to set up:</p><p><span>→ Open Cowork on your desktop</span><br><span>→ Click Dispatch in the sidebar</span><br><span>→ Scan the QR code with the Claude mobile app</span><br><span>→ Send tasks from your phone. Come back to finished work.</span></p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" Your desktop does the work while you are not there.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post">Computer Use<div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§computer-use" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/computer-use" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Claude controls your screen, mouse, and keyboard. It opens apps, navigates browsers, fills in forms. macOS only. Released March 23.</p><p>How to enable:</p><p><span>→ Open Cowork</span><br><span>→ Go to Settings and enable Computer Use</span><br><span>→ Claude asks permission before accessing new apps</span></p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" x2="10" y1="21" y2="14"></line></svg></button></div></div></div></a></figure></div><p>Claude reaches for the most precise tool first. If a Connector exists for Slack, it uses the Connector. If no API or integration exists, it falls back to controlling your screen. Computer Use is the last resort, not the first option.</p><p>It works best for apps and workflows that have no other integration. Internal dashboards, legacy software, anything without an API.</p><p>Tip: Computer Use runs outside the Cowork sandbox. When Claude uses your apps through screen control, it operates on your actual machine. Set up global instructions in Cowork like “never delete files without confirmation” and they carry over to Computer Use sessions.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post">Claude in Excel<div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§claude-in-excel" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/claude-in-excel" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>An add-in that reads your entire workbook. Understands nested formulas, multi-tab dependencies, and provides cell-level citations for every explanation.</p><p>How to install:</p><p><span>→ Open Excel</span><br><span>→ Home → Add-ins</span><br><span>→ Search “Claude by Anthropic”</span><br><span>→ Open sidebar: Ctrl+Alt+C (Windows) or Ctrl+Option+C (Mac)</span></p><p>Ask Claude to trace errors first. “Why is cell B4 showing #REF? Trace the error.” Claude navigates across tabs, finds the broken reference, and fixes it while preserving dependencies. Every change is highlighted with explanatory comments.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" Claude pulls live market data directly into your spreadsheet without leaving Excel. If you have connectors set up in your Claude settings, those same connections work in the add-in automatically.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post">Claude in PowerPoint<div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§claude-in-powerpoint" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/claude-in-powerpoint" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Load your company template and describe what you need. Claude reads your fonts, colours, and layouts, and builds a complete presentation from scratch.</p><div data-component-name="VideoEmbedPlayer" id="media-6c1c5fe4-d143-430a-805a-f4a75d75a0e1" class="videoScrollTarget-SzB20Y"><div class="videoEmbed-_FycLU"><div role="region" aria-label="Video player" class="with-preview video-player-with-background video-player-wrapper"><div class="video-player video-player video-player-with-background videoPlayer-vlcedM" style="padding-bottom: 60%;"><video controlslist="nodownload" preload="metadata" data-video-id="6c1c5fe4-d143-430a-805a-f4a75d75a0e1" poster="https://substack-video.s3.amazonaws.com/video_upload/post/192626545/6c1c5fe4-d143-430a-805a-f4a75d75a0e1/transcoded-00001.png?refresh=Fri Jul 17 2026 17:09:36 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time)" crossorigin="anonymous" class="video-P2qgwZ" src="blob:https://charliehills.substack.com/10b34a36-d6ad-4878-9788-5a9ce40fefad"></video><div class="pencraft pc-position-absolute pc-reset buttonContainer-tH3LP9 video-player-button"><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="c" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft video-player-play-pause-button media-XfKJl4 size_md-gCDS3o priority_primary-RfbeYt iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_md-gCDS3o priority_primary-RfbeYt rounded-SYxRdz" style="width: 72px; height: 72px;"><svg role="img" width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="var(--color-fg-primary)" stroke-width="1.8" stroke="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-label="Play" class="videoIcon-pEKkgy" style="stroke: none;"><g><title></title><path d="M3.35866 16C2.58101 16 2 15.4101 2 14.4447V1.55531C2 0.598883 2.58101 0 3.35866 0C3.75196 0 4.10056 0.134078 4.54749 0.393296L15.1575 6.54302C15.9531 7.00782 16.3106 7.39218 16.3106 8C16.3106 8.61676 15.9531 9.00112 15.1575 9.45698L4.54749 15.6067C4.10056 15.8659 3.75196 16 3.35866 16Z"></path></g></svg></button></div></div></div></div></div><p>How to install:</p><p><span>→ Open PowerPoint</span><br><span>→ Home → Add-ins</span><br><span>→ Search “Claude by Anthropic”</span></p><p>“Create a 10-slide quarterly review using the data from my Excel file.”</p><p>That is one prompt. Claude handles the structure, the copy, the charts, and the formatting. You review and refine.</p><p>Go to Settings in the sidebar and add your Instructions. Tell Claude your brand colours, preferred fonts, and whether you want speaker notes on every slide. It applies these rules to every session automatically.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" x2="10" y1="21" y2="14"></line></svg></button></div></div></div></a></figure></div><p>Toggle on “Let Claude work across files” and Claude references all your open presentations at once. Combined with the Excel add-in, Claude reads your workbook and builds the deck in a single session. No copying. No pasting. No switching between apps.</p><p>Where it goes further is the fine-tuning. Select any slide and tell Claude what to change. “Make this bullet point shorter.” “Convert this table into a bar chart.” “Add a transition slide before the financials.” Claude edits in place and preserves everything around it.</p><p>PowerPoint is not dead. It just needed a better co-pilot.</p><p>Tip: Claude in PowerPoint comes with pre-built Skills you can trigger with a slash command. Type / in the sidebar to see what is available. Skills you have already set up in your Claude account work here too.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" It reads your entire codebase, writes code, runs tests, and ships changes autonomously.</p><p>4% of all GitHub public commits are now authored by Claude Code. That number is projected to hit 20% by year end. Claude Code generates $2.5 billion in annual revenue on its own.</p><p>You do not need to be a developer to understand what Code does. But you do need a terminal to run it.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Installation</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§claudemd" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/claudemd" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>CLAUDE.md is a file that sits in your project folder and loads automatically every time you open Claude Code. It is your standing brief. Claude reads it before doing anything, every session. You never repeat yourself.</p><p>Think of it as the brief you would give a new team member, but you only write it once.</p><p>How to create:</p><p><span>→ Open terminal in your project folder</span><br><span>→ Run /init and Claude generates a CLAUDE.md</span><br><span>→ Edit it to add your own rules, preferences, and context</span></p><p>What to include:</p><p><span>→ Who you are and what you do</span><br><span>→ Your brand voice and style rules</span><br><span>→ The specific project you are working on</span><br><span>→ Formats, tools, and file structures you use</span><br><span>→ Things Claude should never do</span></p><p>Keep it 200 to 500 lines. Front-load the most important instructions. Claude has primacy bias, so what comes first gets weighted most.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" href="https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!HLc3!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8c630973-0de6-4466-8839-e9afb9e5d21d_1080x449.png" That is what </span><a href="https://www.vislo.ai/">Vislo</a><span> is for.</span></p><p>Sign up at vislo.ai before the price goes up.</p><p>Tip: update your CLAUDE.md when Claude repeats a mistake. If it keeps doing something wrong, add a rule at the top of the file. Your CLAUDE.md gets sharper the more you use it.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Slash Commands</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§slash-commands" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/slash-commands" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Type / in Claude Code to see every available command. Here are the ones that matter:</p><p><span>→ /init: Scans your codebase and generates a CLAUDE.md</span><br><span>→ /plan: Creates a step-by-step plan before writing code</span><br><span>→ /compact: Compresses your session to save context</span><br><span>→ /context: Check how much session memory you have used</span><br><span>→ /review: Review code changes before committing</span><br><span>→ /clear: Wipe and start fresh</span><br><span>→ /model: Switch between Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku</span><br><span>→ /doctor: Diagnose issues with your setup</span><br><span>→ /find-skills: Browse and install pre-built Skills</span><br><span>→ /add-dir: Add another folder to your session</span><br><span>→ /agents: View sub-agents running in the background</span><br><span>→ /config: Open your settings</span><br><span>→ /loop: Run a recurring schedule</span><br><span>→ /rewind: Undo last action</span><br><span>→ /permissions: Pre-allow specific commands</span></p><div pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§auto-mode" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/auto-mode" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Every time Claude Code writes a file or runs a command, it asks for your approval. On a small task that is fine. On anything complex, you spend half the session clicking approve on things you have stopped reading.</p><p>Before Auto Mode existed, the only way around this was running:</p><p>claude --dangerously-skip-permissions</p><p>The name tells you everything. It removes all permission checks entirely. Fast, but reckless on a real machine with real files, API keys, and credentials sitting around.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" x2="10" y1="21" y2="14"></line></svg></button></div></div></div></a></figure></div><p>Auto Mode sits between the two. Claude makes permission decisions on your behalf, with a safety classifier reviewing each action before it runs. Safe actions proceed automatically. Risky ones get blocked, and Claude tries a different approach without interrupting you.</p><p>How to enable:</p><p><span>→ Run claude --enable-auto-mode in your terminal</span><br><span>→ Press Shift+Tab to cycle to Auto Mode in your session</span><br><span>→ The current mode shows in the status bar</span></p><p>Tip: Auto Mode does not mean fully unattended. If the classifier repeatedly blocks an action, it will still pause and ask you. Think of it as Claude handling the routine approvals so you only get interrupted when something genuinely needs your attention.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>The Optimal Workflow</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§the-optimal-workflow" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/the-optimal-workflow" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Most people jump straight into prompting. That is the wrong approach.</p><h4 class="header-anchor-post">The workflow:<div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§the-workflow" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/the-workflow" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h4><ol><li><p>/plan: Describe what you want. Review the plan before approving anything.</p></li><li><p>Be specific about the outcome. Name the format, the structure, the file type.</p></li><li><p>Let Claude break it into steps. Read them. This is where you catch misunderstandings before they cost you time.</p></li><li><p>Make one or two changes, then approve.</p></li><li><p>Let Claude run the whole task without interrupting it.</p></li><li><p>Refine the output in the same session.</p></li></ol><p>This is what this looks like in practice. Start simple, then layer in as much as you want.</p><h4 class="header-anchor-post">Step 1: The basic version.<div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§step-1-the-basic-version" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/step-1-the-basic-version" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h4><blockquote><p>/plan Scrape my last 20 LinkedIn posts using Apify, identify the 5 with the highest engagement, and rewrite each one as a 60-second Instagram Reel script. Format each script with a hook, three key points, and a CTA. Save each as a separate file in my Reels folder.</p></blockquote><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" x2="10" y1="21" y2="14"></line></svg></button></div></div></div></a></figure></div><p>Claude builds it to your exact spec. Not Notion, not a generic tool. Yours.</p><p>Each step builds on the last. You run the same basic workflow and add layers depending on how much signal you want feeding into the output.</p><p>The reason all of this works is the CLAUDE.md. Claude already knows your voice, your audience, and your content style before it writes a word. The plan keeps the task scoped. The approval step means no surprises.</p><p>And if the output is not quite right, you do not edit the file manually. You just tell Claude what you did not like in plain language. “The hooks are too long.” “Stop using bullet points in the scripts.” “Always include a posting time suggestion.” Claude updates the CLAUDE.md there and then. Every future run reflects the change.</p><p>Tip: type /effort high before a complex task. Claude reasons more deeply before acting. For quick fixes, leave it on default.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Skills and Automations</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§skills-and-automations" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/skills-and-automations" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>The workflow above took one session to build. Skills turn it into one command you run forever.</p><p>A SKILL.md file defines the entire workflow. Every step, every instruction, every output format. You run it once to build it, then trigger it anytime with a single command.</p><p>So instead of typing that full /plan prompt every week, you type:</p><blockquote><p>/linkedin-to-reels</p></blockquote><p>Claude runs the whole thing. Scrapes your posts, ranks by engagement, writes the scripts, saves the files. Same output every time, without you typing a word.</p><h4 class="header-anchor-post">How to create the Skill from the session you just ran:<div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§how-to-create-the-skill-from-the-session-you-just-ran" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/how-to-create-the-skill-from-the-session-you-just-ran" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h4><p>At the end of any session that produced a workflow you want to repeat, type:</p><blockquote><p>Write a Skill for this workflow.</p></blockquote><p>Claude writes the SKILL.md based on exactly what you both just built together. Save it to ./claude/skills/ and it is ready to run in any future session.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" GitHub, Notion, Slack, Figma, Airtable, and hundreds more. Claude reads, writes, and takes actions across all connected services from your terminal.</p><p>How to connect:</p><p><span>→ Run: claude mcp add --transport http [server-name] [server-url]</span><br><span>→ Browse available servers at https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp</span></p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" Every connected server adds context Claude has to manage in each session. More is not better. Connect the tools you open every day and ignore the rest.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Parallel Agents</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§parallel-agents" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/parallel-agents" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Split tasks across multiple agents running at the same time. Add “use subagents” to your prompt and Claude spawns parallel workers.</p><p>How it works:</p><p><span>→ One orchestrator agent manages the task</span><br><span>→ Sub-agents work on separate components simultaneously</span><br><span>→ Results merge back into the main session</span></p><p>Building on the Reels workflow, this is where it gets fast. Instead of Claude scraping LinkedIn, then scraping Instagram, then researching trending Reels one after another, you add “use subagents” and all three run at the same time.</p><blockquote><p>/plan Use subagents to run these three research streams in parallel: scrape my last 20 LinkedIn posts via Apify, scrape my last 10 Instagram Reels via Apify, and pull the top 20 trending Reels in my niche this week. When all three are done, cross-reference the findings and write the top 5 scripts.</p></blockquote><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" Claude synthesises the results when all three finish. The whole job takes a fraction of the time.</p><p>Tip: combine parallel agents with /plan. Plan the task first, then let Claude decompose it into parallel sub-tasks automatically. This is where Code starts feeling like a team, not a tool.</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post"><strong>Remote Control and Channels</strong><div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§remote-control-and-channels" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/remote-control-and-channels" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>Control your Claude Code session from your phone or another device. Similar to Dispatch in Cowork, but for coding sessions.</p><p><span>→ Remote Control: Monitor and interact with sessions from Desktop, iOS, or Android</span><br><span>→ Channels: Control Claude Code via Discord and Telegram as an alternative to the terminal</span></p><p>With the /linkedin-to-reels workflow running on your desktop, Remote Control means you can kick it off from your phone on the way to a meeting and check in on progress without sitting at your Mac. If something needs steering mid-run, you handle it from wherever you are.</p><div class="captioned-image-container"><figure><a target="_blank" You message the bot, it scrapes your LinkedIn posts, asks a couple of quick questions about format and length, and delivers a finished Reel script straight into the chat. Two messages. Done.</p><p>That is one example. The broader point is that once Claude Code is set up, almost anything repetitive, automatable, or delegatable can be handled this way. You do not need to be at your desk. You do not need to open a terminal. You send a message and come back to finished work.</p><p>If you have a task that needs automating, there is a good chance Claude Code can do it.</p><p>Tip: If you manage a team, run Claude Code from a shared Discord or Telegram channel. Everyone sees what Claude is building without needing terminal access.</p><div><hr></div><p>For reference, every major Claude release this year:</p><p><span>→ Jan 7: Claude Code 2.1.0 shipped</span><br><span>→ Jan 12: Cowork launched (Mac, Max plan)</span><br><span>→ Jan 16: Cowork opened to Pro users</span><br><span>→ Feb 5: Opus 4.6 launched (1M context)</span><br><span>→ Feb 17: Sonnet 4.6 launched</span><br><span>→ Feb 19: Claude in PowerPoint launched</span><br><span>→ Feb 20: Claude Code Security launched</span><br><span>→ Feb 24: Remote Control launched</span><br><span>→ Feb 24: Plugin marketplace launched</span><br><span>→ Feb 25: Scheduled Tasks in Cowork</span><br><span>→ Mar 2: Memory became free for all users</span><br><span>→ Mar 6: Enterprise Marketplace launched</span><br><span>→ Mar 11: Excel and PowerPoint sync</span><br><span>→ Mar 12: Interactive charts in chat</span><br><span>→ Mar 17: Dispatch (phone to desktop)</span><br><span>→ Mar 23: Computer Use went live (macOS)</span><br><span>→ Mar 24: Claude Code Auto Mode launched</span></p><p>17 releases in under 3 months. And they are not slowing down.</p><p>Most people read a list like that and feel behind. I want you to feel the opposite. Every feature in this newsletter is available to you today. The gap between where you are and where you could be is not about access. It is about setup.</p><p>Pick one thing from this newsletter and set it up this week.</p><p>The people pulling the furthest ahead are not using some secret version of Claude. They are using more of the product than everyone else.</p><p>This is the most comprehensive breakdown of Claude I have published. If it saved you time or changed how you think about the product, share it with someone who is still typing into a chat box and calling it an AI strategy.</p><p><em>Stay curious, stay human, and start treating Claude like the operating system it has become.</em></p><p>— Charlie</p><div><hr></div><h3 class="header-anchor-post">Events<div class="pencraft pc-display-flex pc-alignItems-center pc-position-absolute pc-reset header-anchor-parent"><div class="pencraft pc-display-contents pc-reset pubTheme-yiXxQA"><div id="§events" class="pencraft pc-reset header-anchor offset-top"></div><button tabindex="0" type="button" aria-label="Link" data-href="https://charliehills.substack.com/i/192626545/events" class="pencraft pc-reset pencraft iconButton-mq_Et5 iconButtonBase-dJGHgN buttonBase-GK1x3M buttonStyle-r7yGCK size_sm-G3LciD priority_secondary-S63h9o"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-link"><path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path><path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path></svg></button></div></div></h3><p>If you want to meet in person, I have two coming up.</p><p><a href="https://www.influencerstrategists.com/creator-events/fuji">AI in the Creator Economy</a><span> is on April 9 at the FUJIFILM House of Photography. a chatbot.</p><p>Claude is three products. Not one.</p><p><strong>Chat</strong> is where you think. <strong>Cowork</strong> is where you produce. <strong>Code</strong> is where you automate.</p><p>Each product has its own set of features. Most people know Chat. Some have opened Cowork. Almost nobody outside of developers has touched Code.</p><p>Today I am walking through all three. Every feature. How to turn it on. How I use it.</p><div><hr></div><h2><strong>Claude Chat</strong></h2><p>This is where everyone starts.</p><p>1. Go to claude.ai<br>2. Open the app<br>3. Type\u2026</p><p>But Chat has 12 built-in tools. Most people use 3.</p><p>Here is the full list:</p><div><hr></div><h3><strong>Models + Extended Thinking</strong></h3><p><strong>Opus 4.6</strong> is the most capable model. 1M token context window. Use it for strategy, legal analysis, financial modelling, and anything with multiple variables.</p><p><strong>Sonnet 4.6</strong> handles 90% of your daily work. Faster. Cheaper. I use this for everything unless I have a reason not to.</p><p><strong>Haiku 4.5</strong> is the lightweight option. Fast and cheap. Good for quick answers and bulk tasks where depth does not matter.</p><p>My rule: start with Sonnet. Switch to Opus when the task demands it. Most people leave Opus on permanently and burn through their usage limit by Wednesday.</p><p>Below the model selector you will see Extended Thinking. Toggle it on. Claude reasons through your problem step by step before responding. Shows its working. Breaks complex questions into parts.</p><div class=\\\"captioned-image-container\\\"><figure><a class=\\\"image-link image2 is-viewable-img\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\" Thinking on. The difference is like asking someone to answer immediately versus giving them 5 minutes to think. Turn on Extended Thinking and Web Search at the same time. Two clicks that change everything.</p><p>How to access: model dropdown at the top of any conversation.</p><div><hr></div><h3><strong>Memory</strong></h3><p>Claude remembers you across conversations. Free for all users since March 2026.</p><p>Two settings to enable in Settings \u2192 Capabilities:</p><p><strong>Search and reference chats </strong>\u2192 Claude searches your past conversations for relevant context.</p><p><strong>Generate memory from chat history </strong>\u2192 Claude remembers details about you across all chats and Projects.</p><p>Turn both on. Go to Settings \u2192 Capabilities \u2192 Memory and click Start Import. Claude gives you a prompt to fetch your memory from your other account. I wrote a full guide on how to do this, linked below.</p><div class=\\\"digest-post-embed\\\" data-attrs=\\\"{"nodeId":"d5118f43-612b-4cce-b359-339d8e38bf81","caption":"You have spent months teaching ChatGPT who you are. Old projects you are no longer working on, incorrect assumptions about your role, outdated preferences. Go into your memory, read through it, and remove anything that is wrong or stale.</p><div class=\\\"captioned-image-container\\\"><figure><a class=\\\"image-link image2 is-viewable-img\\\" document. Review it every few weeks. Remove what is outdated. Add what is missing. The cleaner your memory, the less time you spend correcting Claude in every conversation.</p><div><hr></div><h3><strong>Web Search</strong></h3><p>Claude searches the web mid-conversation and pulls live data into its response.</p><p>Two ways to trigger it:</p><p>\u2192 Toggle it on in Settings (stays on for every chat)<br>\u2192 Or type \u201Csearch the web for...\u201D in your prompt (one-off)</p><p>I keep mine toggled off by default to save tokens. When I need it, I type \u201Csearch the web\u201D directly in my message. That\u2019s faster than enabling it, and I only burn the tokens when the task needs live data.</p><div class=\\\"captioned-image-container\\\"><figure><a class=\\\"image-link image2 for your most complex questions. Claude pulls live data AND reasons through it step by step. Two toggles that change the quality of every response.</p><div><hr></div><h3><strong>Deep Research</strong></h3><p>This is different from Web Search. Deep Research runs a full multi-source investigation. Claude searches dozens of sources, cross-references them, and returns a structured report.</p><p>How to use:</p><p>\u2192 Click the Research button next to the message bar<br>\u2192 Enter your topic<br>\u2192 Wait for the full report (takes a few minutes)</p><p>I sometimes use it before every newsletter. I give it a topic, it comes back with data points, competitor examples, and source links.</p><div class=\\\"captioned-image-container\\\"><figure><a class=\\\"image-link image2 is-viewable-img\\\" you to choose between options and given you buttons instead of a text list.</p><p>Two ways to trigger it:</p><p>\u2192 Claude decides to use it automatically<br>\u2192 Or type \u201CAskUserQuestion:\u201D in your prompt to force it</p><p>\u201CWhich format do you want?\u201D \u2192 clickable buttons.<br>\u201CRank these in order of priority\u201D \u2192 drag and drop.<br>\u201CWhich of these apply?\u201D \u2192 multi-select checkboxes.</p><p>Tip: if Claude asks you a question in plain text and you would prefer clickable options, say \u201Cgive me options to choose from.\u201D Claude switches to the interactive format. Works the other way too. If you want to type a freeform answer instead of picking from options, ignore the buttons and type your response.</p><div><hr></div><h3><strong>Projects</strong></h3><p>Save files and instructions once. Every new chat inside a Project starts with full context. Claude reads everything before you type a word.</p><div class=\\\"captioned-image-container\\\"><figure><a class=\\\"image-link image2 is-viewable-img\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\" \u201CCreate Project\u201D in the left sidebar<br>\u2192 Upload your key files and write custom instructions<br>\u2192 Start new chats inside the Project</p><p>I run 12 active Projects. One per client, one for this newsletter, one for LinkedIn content. Each has a custom instruction, 3 to 5 reference files, and zero filler.</p><p>Most people do everything in one Project. I do the opposite.</p><p>I take a piece of content and move it through different Projects depending on what I need. A newsletter becomes a LinkedIn post, which becomes an infographic, which becomes an email subject line.</p><p>Each Project has its own custom instructions, tone rules, and reference files. The output is tailored every time without me re-explaining anything.</p><p>I switch between Projects throughout the day depending on the task.</p><div You do not need to type out a SKILL.md file yourself.</p><p>Here is how it works in practice:</p><p>\u2192 Explain the problem you are trying to solve<br>\u2192 Provide reference material of what you want<br>\u2192 Claude creates the Skill for you</p><p>Then you test it and refine it over time through natural language in chat. \u201CMake the tone more casual.\u201D \u201CAdd a step where you check Skill. I showed Claude a few examples of the style I wanted, described the dimensions and brand colours, and he wrote the full Skill. I have refined it a few times since, through conversation, and now it produces consistent results every time I run it.</p><p><strong>Advanced: chaining multiple Skills inside a Project</strong></p><p>You can set up a Project that calls multiple Skills as part of one process.</p><p>\u2192 Create separate Skills for each stage (researcher, writer, reviewer)<br>\u2192 Upload the empty SKILL.md files into your Project<br>\u2192 Tell Claude in the Project instructions which Skill to call at each step</p><p>One prompt kicks off the entire chain. Claude researches, writes, and reviews, all following different Skills in sequence.</p><p>I do not personally follow this approach yet, but my co-founder at <a href=\\\"http://www.linked-agency.com\\\">Linked Agency </a>recently started using it for client work and it has been a standout for outsourcing repetitive deliverables where the process stays the same but the content changes each week.</p><div><hr></div><h3><strong>Connectors (MCP)</strong></h3><p>Connect Slack, Google Drive, Notion, Gmail, and 50+ tools. Claude searches them mid-chat.</p><p>How to connect:</p><p>\u2192 Click the connectors icon in any chat<br>\u2192 Browse and authorise the tools you use<br>\u2192 Claude pulls context without you Weekly browser reports, daily form checks, regular data pulls from dashboards that have no API.</p><p>Tip: Start by recording a simple task you do every week in the browser. Something repetitive that takes 5 minutes. Once you see Claude replay it without you touching anything, you will find more things to record.</p><div><hr></div><h2><strong>Claude Cowork</strong></h2><p>Cowork is where Claude moves from answering questions to doing work on your machine. It reads your files, creates real documents, and delivers finished outputs into your folders.</p><p>If Chat is the brain, Cowork is the hands.</p><p>You need the Claude Desktop app. Mac and Windows. Pro and Max plans.</p><p>\u2192 Open Claude Desktop.<br>\u2192 Click the Cowork tab.</p><p>That is your starting point.</p><div class=\\\"captioned-image-container\\\"><figure><a class=\\\"image-link Large tasks with independent components<br>\u2192 Research across multiple sources at once<br>\u2192 Anything sequential that takes too long</p><p>Tip: be specific about the output you want upfront. There is less back and forth when sub-agents are running in parallel, so the clarity needs to be in the original prompt.</p><div><hr></div><h3>Plugins</h3><p>Skills are individual instruction files. Plugins bundle multiple Skills, connectors, slash commands, and sub-agents into one installable package for an entire role.</p><p>A sales Plugin connects to your CRM, adds prospecting workflows, and gives you /research and /outreach commands. A legal Plugin includes your NDA playbook, document management connector, and /triage-nda.</p><p>Cowork ships with 11 pre-built Plugins covering sales, finance, legal, marketing, HR, engineering, and more.</p><p>Four ways to add them:</p><p><strong>Browse plugins</strong> \u2192 Pre-built library, one-click install.<br><strong>Add marketplace</strong> \u2192 Your organisation\u2019s private plugins (Team/Enterprise).<br><strong>Upload plugin</strong> \u2192 Drop in one a colleague built.<br><strong>Create with Claude</strong> \u2192 Describe what you want, Claude builds it.</p><div Monday at 8am, summarise my unread Slack messages and flag anything urgent.\u201D Once it works, add a second.</p><p>Build the habit of delegating one recurring task before trying to automate everything at once.</p><p>Where it gets interesting is when you bring Connectors into it.</p><p>Connect your Slack, Google Drive, or Notion, and your Scheduled Tasks stop being static prompts. pre-configured Apify scrapers across Instagram, LinkedIn, X, TikTok, Threads, Facebook, and YouTube. It collects the 5 most recent posts from each account I track, including captions and engagement metrics, and then compiles everything into a daily content briefing. I open my laptop and it is already done.</p><p>One caveat: your computer needs to be awake for Scheduled Tasks to run. Set them for a time when your laptop is already open, your morning routine, a lunch break, the end of your working day.</p><div><hr></div><h3>Dispatch</h3><p>Assign Claude a task from your phone. It runs on your desktop while you are away and messages you when the work is done. One continuous conversation across your phone and your computer.</p><p>How to set up:</p><p>\u2192 Open Cowork on your desktop<br>\u2192 Click Dispatch in the sidebar<br>\u2192 Scan the QR code with the Claude mobile app<br>\u2192 Send tasks from your phone. It opens apps, navigates browsers, fills in forms. macOS only. Released March 23.</p><p>How to enable:</p><p>\u2192 Open Cowork<br>\u2192 Go to Settings and enable Computer Use<br>\u2192 Claude asks permission before accessing new apps</p><div first. If a Connector exists for Slack, it uses the Connector. If no API or integration exists, it falls back to controlling your screen. Computer Use is the last resort, not the first option.</p><p>It works best for apps and workflows that have no other integration. Internal dashboards, legacy software, anything without an API.</p><p>Tip: Computer Use runs outside the Cowork sandbox. When Claude uses your apps through screen control, it operates on your actual machine. Set up global instructions in Cowork like \u201Cnever delete files without confirmation\u201D and they carry over to Computer Use sessions.</p><div><hr></div><h3>Claude in Excel</h3><p>An add-in that reads your entire workbook. Understands nested formulas, multi-tab dependencies, and provides cell-level citations for every explanation.</p><p>How to install:</p><p>\u2192 Open Excel<br>\u2192 Home \u2192 Add-ins<br>\u2192 Search \u201CClaude by Anthropic\u201D<br>\u2192 Open sidebar: Ctrl+Alt+C (Windows) or Ctrl+Option+C (Mac)</p><p>Ask Claude to trace errors first. \u201CWhy is cell B4 showing #REF? Trace the error.\u201D Claude navigates across tabs, finds the broken reference, and fixes it while preserving dependencies. Every change is highlighted with explanatory comments.</p><div class=\\\"captioned-image-container\\\"><figure><a class=\\\"image-link image2 is-viewable-img\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\" If you have connectors set up in your Claude settings, those same connections work in the add-in automatically.</p><div><hr></div><h3>Claude in PowerPoint</h3><p>Load your company template and describe what you need. Claude reads your fonts, colours, and layouts, and builds a complete presentation from scratch.</p><div class=\\\"native-video-embed\\\" data-component-name=\\\"VideoPlaceholder\\\" data-attrs=\\\"{"mediaUploadId":"6c1c5fe4-d143-430a-805a-f4a75d75a0e1","duration":null}\\\"></div><p>How to install:</p><p>\u2192 Open PowerPoint<br>\u2192 Home \u2192 Add-ins<br>\u2192 Search \u201CClaude by Anthropic\u201D</p><p>\u201CCreate a 10-slide quarterly review using the data from my Excel file.\u201D</p><p>That is one prompt. Claude handles the structure, the copy, the charts, and the formatting. You review and refine.</p><p>Go to Settings in the sidebar and add your Instructions. Tell Claude your brand colours, preferred fonts, and whether you want speaker notes on every slide. files\u201D and Claude references all your open presentations at once. Combined with the Excel add-in, Claude reads your workbook and builds the deck in a single session. No copying. No pasting. No switching between apps.</p><p>Where it goes further is the fine-tuning. Select any slide and tell Claude what to change. \u201CMake this bullet point shorter.\u201D \u201CConvert this table into a bar chart.\u201D \u201CAdd a transition slide before the financials.\u201D Claude edits in place and preserves everything around it.</p><p>PowerPoint is not dead. It just needed a better co-pilot.</p><p>Tip: Claude in PowerPoint comes with pre-built Skills you can trigger with a slash command. Type / in the sidebar to see what is available. Skills you have already set up in your Claude account work here too.</p><div class=\\\"captioned-image-container\\\"><figure><a class=\\\"image-link image2 is-viewable-img\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\" Code runs in your terminal. It reads your entire codebase, writes code, runs tests, and ships changes autonomously.</p><p>4% of all GitHub public commits are now authored by Claude Code. That number is projected to hit 20% by year end. Claude Code generates $2.5 billion in annual revenue on its own.</p><p>You do not need to be a developer to understand what Code does. But you do need a terminal to run it.</p><div><hr></div><h3><strong>Installation</strong></h3><p>Three options:</p><p>\u2192 Mac/Linux: curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | sh<br>\u2192 Windows: irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex<br>\u2192 VS Code: Extensions \u2192 search \u201CClaude Code\u201D \u2192 install \u2192 click the Claude icon in the sidebar</p><div y2=\\\"14\\\"></line></svg></button></div></div></div></a></figure></div><div><hr></div><h3><strong>CLAUDE.md</strong></h3><p>CLAUDE.md is a file that sits in your project folder and loads automatically every time you open Claude Code. It is your standing brief. Claude reads it before doing anything, every session. You never repeat yourself.</p><p>Think of it as the brief you would give a new team member, but you only write it once.</p><p>How to create:</p><p>\u2192 Open terminal in your project folder<br>\u2192 Run /init and Claude generates a CLAUDE.md<br>\u2192 Edit it to add your own rules, preferences, and context</p><p>What to include:</p><p>\u2192 Who you are and what you do<br>\u2192 Your brand voice and style rules<br>\u2192 The specific project you are working on<br>\u2192 Formats, tools, and file structures you use<br>\u2192 Things Claude should never do</p><p>Keep it 200 to 500 lines. Front-load the most important instructions. Claude has primacy bias, so what comes first gets weighted most.</p><div class=\\\"captioned-image-container\\\"><figure><a class=\\\"image-link image2 is-viewable-img\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\"
